author: Авакумов Данил Эдуардович
objective: Empirically validate that emergent role weights are proportional to information contribution of each role to the task solution.
principle_under_test: In cooperative evolutionary systems, role weight converges to normalized information contribution of that role.

hypothesis.statement: Emergent role weights match normalized mutual information between role outputs and final task success.
hypothesis.formal_law: w_r ≈ I(role_r ; task_outcome) / Σ I(role_k ; task_outcome)

key_findings: Emergent architecture depends on task structure.
Algorithmic parameters do not change architecture.
Weight-law describes equilibrium but is not causal.
Task structure explains ~99.7% of architecture variance.

analysis_pipeline.title: ROLE SIGNAL EXTRACTION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Extract predictor, critic and aggregator signals from evolutionary runs.

analysis_pipeline.title: INFORMATION METRIC COMPUTATION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Compute mutual information and conditional mutual information for each role.

analysis_pipeline.title: NORMALIZED INFORMATION CONTRIBUTION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Normalize information contributions to produce theoretical role weights.

analysis_pipeline.title: EMPIRICAL VS THEORETICAL COMPARISON
analysis_pipeline.goal: Compare emergent role ratios with information-derived weights.

analysis_pipeline.title: LAW VALIDATION
analysis_pipeline.goal: Validate whether role weight matches normalized information contribution.

analysis_pipeline.title: FINAL REPORT
analysis_pipeline.goal: Produce formal validation report for the information law.

definition_of_done: Empirical role ratios compared with theoretical predictions.
